body: pdf-to-md: swapped the VLM fallback for a smaller open model. Faster and cheaper, but it loses small-caps as italics about a third of the time. Reverting.
Set up runner to summarize my open browser tabs every morning at 7. Costs about a nickel a day. The summaries are mediocre but I keep reading them.
dmscreen now auto-rolls concentration saves on damage. Took two evenings and a session where I nearly killed a PC by mis-rolling.
Tried boosting recency in notesearch. The math was right; the results got worse. Stale notes are more useful when surfaced more, not less.
Ported dmscreen's dice roller to WebAssembly to see if it'd be faster. It is not. Reverted in 20 minutes.
feedpipe was collapsing distinct writers riffing on the same article. Loosened the MinHash threshold from 0.62 to 0.78 and added a “related” cluster instead of merging.
Spent a Saturday on a sentiment analyzer for my git commit messages. Works, kind of. Not sure I want to know.
5e-monster-builder: switched from DMG-as-written CR to the defensive/offensive split. My encounters stopped being trivial.
